input.description: Read helper publish workflow + electron-builder config

content.content:
===== publish-popdam-helper.yml =====
name: Publish PopDAM Helper

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "apps/popdam-helper/**"
      - ".github/workflows/publish-popdam-helper.yml"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  # ── Windows — Electron Builder ────────────────────────────────────
  build-windows:
    name: Build Windows helper
    runs-on: windows-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: apps/popdam-helper

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      # Cache the Electron binary so it isn't re-downloaded on every run.
      # A missing or partially downloaded Electron distribution is the most
      # common cause of "ffmpeg.dll was not found" on Windows.
      - name: Cache Electron
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~\AppData\Local\electron\Cache
            ~\AppData\Local\electron-builder\Cache
          key: electron-win-${{ hashFiles('apps/popdam-helper/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: electron-win-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build TypeScript + Vite
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package with electron-builder (x64 only)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: "false"
          # No Windows code signing cert — auto-update still works but SmartScreen warns on install
        run: npx electron-builder --win --x64 --publish never

      - name: List release artifacts
        shell: powershell
        run: Get-ChildItem release -Recurse | Select-Object FullName, Length

      - name: Upload Windows installer
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: popdam-helper-latest
          name: POP DAM Helper (latest)
          make_latest: true
          body: |
            ## Downloads

            | Platform | File | Notes |
            |----------|------|-------|
            | **Windows 10/11** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Windows-Setup.exe` | Run the installer, then launch from Start Menu |
            | **macOS (Apple Silicon M1/M2/M3)** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-arm64.dmg` | Open DMG, drag to Applications |
            | **macOS (Intel)** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-x64.dmg` | Open DMG, drag to Applications |

            > **Not sure which Mac DMG?** M1 or later → arm64. Older Intel Mac → x64.
            > The two "Source code" archives at the bottom are auto-generated by GitHub — ignore them.

            After installing, open the app once to complete one-time setup: set your workspace folder and map your NAS roots in Settings.
          files: |
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Windows-Setup.exe
            apps/popdam-helper/release/latest.yml
          prerelease: false

  # ── macOS — Electron Builder ──────────────────────────────────────
  build-mac:
    name: Build Mac helper
    runs-on: macos-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: apps/popdam-helper

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Cache Electron
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/Library/Caches/electron
            ~/Library/Caches/electron-builder
          key: electron-mac-${{ hashFiles('apps/popdam-helper/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: electron-mac-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build TypeScript + Vite
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package with electron-builder
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: "false"
          # Add APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID secrets to enable notarization.
          # The afterSign hook in scripts/notarize.cjs skips silently if APPLE_ID is not set.
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: npx electron-builder --mac --publish never

      - name: List release artifacts
        run: find release -type f | head -20

      - name: Upload macOS installers
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: popdam-helper-latest
          name: POP DAM Helper (latest)
          make_latest: true
          files: |
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-arm64.dmg
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-x64.dmg
            apps/popdam-helper/release/latest-mac.yml
          prerelease: false

===== electron-builder.yml (mac/win/publish blocks) =====
appId: com.popcreations.popdam-helper
productName: POP DAM Helper
copyright: Copyright © 2026 POP Creations

directories:
  buildResources: build
  output: release

# Copy resources/ into the packaged app so process.resourcesPath resolves them
extraResources:
  - from: "resources/"
    to: "."
    filter: ["**/*"]

files:
  - out/**
  - package.json
  - "!**/.vscode/*"
  - "!src/*"
  - "!electron.vite.config.{js,ts,mjs,cjs}"
  - "!{.eslintignore,.eslintrc.cjs,.prettierignore,.prettierrc.yaml,dev-app-update.yml,CHANGELOG.md,README.md}"
  - "!{.env,.env.*,.npmrc,pnpm-lock.yaml}"
  - "!{tsconfig,tsconfig.*}.json"

afterSign: scripts/notarize.cjs

win:
  executableName: popdam-helper
  icon: resources/icon.png
  artifactName: "POP-DAM-Helper-Windows-Setup.exe"
  target:
    - target: nsis
      arch:
        - x64

nsis:
  shortcutName: POP DAM Helper
  uninstallDisplayName: POP DAM Helper
  createDesktopShortcut: false
  createStartMenuShortcut: true
  runAfterFinish: true

mac:
  hardenedRuntime: true
  gatekeeperAssess: false
  entitlements: build/entitlements.mac.plist
  entitlementsInherit: build/entitlements.mac.plist
  extendInfo:
    - NSCameraUsageDescription: Application requests access to the device's camera.
    - NSMicrophoneUsageDescription: Application requests access to the device's microphone.
    - NSDocumentsFolderUsageDescription: Application requests access to the user's Documents folder.
    - NSDownloadsFolderUsageDescription: Application requests access to the user's Downloads folder.
  icon: resources/icon.png
  target:
    - target: dmg
      arch:
        - x64
        - arm64

dmg:
  artifactName: "POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-${arch}.dmg"

protocols:
  - name: POP DAM Helper
    schemes:
      - popdam

publish:
  provider: github
  owner: u2giants
  repo: popdam3
  releaseType: release

===== entitlements =====
entitlements.mac.plist
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit</key>
    <true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory</key>
    <true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables</key>
    <true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.network.client</key>
    <true/>
    <key>com.apple.security.files.user-selected.read-write</key>
    <true/>
</dict>
</plist>

        uses: actions/cache@v4

          path: |
            ~\AppData\Local\electron\Cache
            ~\AppData\Local\electron-builder\Cache
          key: electron-win-${{ hashFiles('apps/popdam-helper/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: electron-win-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build TypeScript + Vite
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package with electron-builder (x64 only)
        env:

        run: npx electron-builder --win --x64 --publish never

          tag_name: popdam-helper-latest
          name: POP DAM Helper (latest)
          make_latest: true
          body: |
            ## Downloads

            | Platform | File | Notes |
            |----------|------|-------|
            | **Windows 10/11** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Windows-Setup.exe` | Run the installer, then launch from Start Menu |
            | **macOS (Apple Silicon M1/M2/M3)** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-arm64.dmg` | Open DMG, drag to Applications |
            | **macOS (Intel)** | `POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-x64.dmg` | Open DMG, drag to Applications |

            > **Not sure which Mac DMG?** M1 or later → arm64. Older Intel Mac → x64.
            > The two "Source code" archives at the bottom are auto-generated by GitHub — ignore them.

            After installing, open the app once to complete one-time setup: set your workspace folder and map your NAS roots in Settings.
          files: |
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Windows-Setup.exe
            apps/popdam-helper/release/latest.yml
          prerelease: false

  # ── macOS — Electron Builder ──────────────────────────────────────
  build-mac:
    name: Build Mac helper
    runs-on: macos-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: apps/popdam-helper

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Cache Electron
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/Library/Caches/electron
            ~/Library/Caches/electron-builder
          key: electron-mac-${{ hashFiles('apps/popdam-helper/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: electron-mac-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build TypeScript + Vite
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package with electron-builder
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: "false"
          # Add APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID secrets to enable notarization.
          # The afterSign hook in scripts/notarize.cjs skips silently if APPLE_ID is not set.
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: npx electron-builder --mac --publish never

      - name: List release artifacts
        run: find release -type f | head -20

      - name: Upload macOS installers
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: popdam-helper-latest
          name: POP DAM Helper (latest)
          make_latest: true
          files: |
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-arm64.dmg
            apps/popdam-helper/release/POP-DAM-Helper-Mac-x64.dmg
            apps/popdam-helper/release/latest-mac.yml
          prerelease: false

Done — repo wiring for macOS signing is pushed (`9d134e8`). Here are both deliverables.

## 1) Prompt for the Claude running on the Seafile box

Paste this verbatim:

> **Task: Switch Seafile to real Microsoft (Entra) OAuth SSO and remove the insecure auto-login SSO.**
>
> Context: This is the Seafile Pro server for `seafile.designflow.app`. Right now clicking "Single Sign-On" logs a user straight in with **no Microsoft prompt** — that means SSO is misconfigured (likely a `REMOTE_USER` / reverse-proxy header trust, or a stale provider), which is a security hole. The Entra app registration is already done; only the server config (`seahub_settings.py`) is wrong.
>
> Do this:
> 1. Locate the live config: `sudo find / -name seahub_settings.py 2>/dev/null` (Docker installs keep it in the seafile data volume, e.g. `/opt/seafile-data/seafile/conf/` or `/shared/seafile/conf/`). Confirm which file the running server actually loads.
> 2. **Back it up**: `cp seahub_settings.py seahub_settings.py.bak.$(date +%s)`.
> 3. **Show me** the current SSO-related lines before changing anything — anything with `REMOTE_USER`, `ENABLE_REMOTE_USER_AUTHENTICATION`, `ENABLE_SHIBBOLETH`, `SHIBBOLETH_*`, `ENABLE_OAUTH`, `OAUTH_*`, or `EXTRA_MIDDLEWARE`. Identify which setting is causing the no-prompt auto-login and **remove/disable only that**.
> 4. Add this OAuth block (this is the real, already-registered Entra app). Get the `OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` value from me — it's the value of the Entra secret named `seafile-oauth` (or I'll mint a fresh one in Entra → Certificates & secrets, since Azure won't show the existing value):
> ```python
> ENABLE_OAUTH = True
> OAUTH_ENABLE_INSECURE_TRANSPORT = False
> OAUTH_CLIENT_ID = "8d9da03c-e5cd-4a23-b987-32aaaed31fe7"
> OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET = "<<ASK ME FOR THIS>>"
> OAUTH_REDIRECT_URL = "https://seafile.designflow.app/oauth/callback/"
> OAUTH_PROVIDER_DOMAIN = "designflow.app"
> TENANT = "1caeb1c0-a087-4cb9-b046-a5e22404f971"
> OAUTH_AUTHORIZATION_URL = f"https://login.microsoftonline.com/{TENANT}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize"
> OAUTH_TOKEN_URL         = f"https://login.microsoftonline.com/{TENANT}/oauth2/v2.0/token"
> OAUTH_USER_INFO_URL     = "https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo"
> OAUTH_SCOPE = ["openid", "profile", "email"]
> OAUTH_ATTRIBUTE_MAP = {
>     "sub":   (True,  "uid"),
>     "email": (False, "contact_email"),
>     "name":  (False, "name"),
> }
> ```
> 5. Restart so it reloads: Docker → `docker restart seafile` (use the real container name); native → `./seahub.sh restart`. Watch the logs for config/import errors.
> 6. **Verify**: open `https://seafile.designflow.app` in a fresh private window, click Single Sign-On, and confirm you now get a **real Microsoft login prompt** and land in Seafile afterward. Report back what happened, and confirm the old auto-login no longer works.
> 7. If anything errors, restore the `.bak` and tell me what broke. Keep secrets out of any output you share.

## 2) Getting the Helper signed (you have an Apple Developer account)

I already did the **repo side** (`9d134e8`): the Mac build now uses `CSC_LINK`/`CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` and will sign + notarize as soon as the secrets exist. You just need to create the cert and add 5 GitHub secrets.

**On a Mac:**
1. **Create the cert** — it must be **"Developer ID Application"** (for distribution *outside* the App Store; *not* "Apple Distribution"). Easiest: Xcode → Settings → Accounts → your Apple ID → **Manage Certificates → + → Developer ID Application**.
2. **Export it to .p12** — Keychain Access → My Certificates → right-click *"Developer ID Application: … (TEAMID)"* → **Export** → save as `.p12` → set an export password (remember it).
3. **base64-encode it**: `base64 -i Certificates.p12 | pbcopy`
4. **App-specific password** (for notarization): [appleid.apple.com](https://appleid.apple.com) → Sign-In and Security → **App-Specific Passwords** → generate one.
5. **Team ID**: developer.apple.com → **Membership** → 10-character Team ID.

**Then in GitHub** (repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret), add **5 secrets**:

| Secret | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `CSC_LINK` | the base64 string from step 3 |
| `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` | the .p12 export password from step 2 |
| `APPLE_ID` | your Apple ID email |
| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | the app-specific password (step 4) |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | your Team ID (step 5) |

**Trigger a build:** Actions → **Publish PopDAM Helper → Run workflow** (or push any change under `apps/popdam-helper/`). The Mac job will now sign + notarize → the DMG installs with **no Gatekeeper warning** and **auto-update works**.

Two notes:
- This covers **macOS only**. Windows SmartScreen is a separate OV/EV cert (you didn't ask, so I left the Windows job alone).
- The build I just triggered with the wiring commit will still be **unsigned** (secrets aren't there yet) — that's expected; the first *signed* build is your next run after adding the secrets.

message.content: I don't own a Mac, is there another way to do it?

content.text:
No Mac needed at all. Two reasons:
1. The **actual signing + notarization happens on GitHub's `macos-latest` runner** (the CI), not on your machine — you only need to *produce* the certificate file.
2. You can create the certificate with **OpenSSL** on Windows or Linux — Keychain/Xcode is just Apple's convenience path, not a requirement.

OpenSSL is on Windows via Git Bash/WSL, or any Linux box. Here's the full no-Mac flow:

### A) Create the cert with OpenSSL
```bash
# 1. Generate a private key (KEEP THIS FILE SAFE — it's half your signing identity)
openssl genrsa -out popdam_key.pem 2048

# 2. Generate a Certificate Signing Request
openssl req -new -key popdam_key.pem -out popdam.csr \
  -subj "/emailAddress=you@popcre.com/CN=POP Creations/C=US"
```

### B) Get the cert from Apple (web only)
- developer.apple.com → **Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles → Certificates → +**
- Choose **"Developer ID Application"** (under *Software* — **not** "Apple Distribution"; that's App Store only)
- Upload `popdam.csr`, **Download** the resulting `developerID_application.cer`
- ⚠️ This cert type can only be created by the **Account Holder** of the Apple Developer account (not a regular Admin/Member). If that's you, you're fine.

### C) Bundle into a .p12 with OpenSSL
```bash
# convert Apple's DER cert to PEM
openssl x509 -inform DER -in developerID_application.cer -out popdam_cert.pem

# (recommended) grab Apple's intermediate so the chain is complete:
curl -O https://www.apple.com/certificateauthority/DeveloperIDG2CA.cer
openssl x509 -inform DER -in DeveloperIDG2CA.cer -out apple_intermediate.pem

# build the .p12 — you'll be prompted for an export password (remember it)
openssl pkcs12 -export -out popdam.p12 \
  -inkey popdam_key.pem -in popdam_cert.pem \
  -certfile apple_intermediate.pem \
  -name "Developer ID Application"

# base64-encode for the GitHub secret
base64 -w0 popdam.p12 > popdam.p12.b64    # Windows Git Bash: base64 popdam.p12 > popdam.p12.b64
```

### D) Add the same 5 GitHub secrets
| Secret | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `CSC_LINK` | contents of `popdam.p12.b64` |
| `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` | the export password from step C |
| `APPLE_ID` | your Apple ID email |
| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | app-specific password from [appleid.apple.com](https://appleid.apple.com) |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | 10-char Team ID (developer.apple.com → Membership) |

Then **Actions → Publish PopDAM Helper → Run workflow**. The Mac runner imports your `.p12`, signs, and notarizes — entirely in the cloud.

A couple of notes:
- **Don't lose `popdam_key.pem`** — if you ever need to rebuild the `.p12` you need it; if it leaks, revoke the cert in the portal and redo.
- I'd suggest you run the OpenSSL commands **on your own machine**, not ask me to — that private key is your signing identity and shouldn't pass through this session. I've already done all the repo-side wiring, so once the 5 secrets are in, it just works.

content.text:
You already have OpenSSL — it ships **inside Git for Windows**, it's just not on PowerShell's PATH. Easiest fix: use **Git Bash** instead of PowerShell.

### Open Git Bash
Start menu → type **"Git Bash"** → open it. OpenSSL works there directly. (If "Git Bash" isn't there, install OpenSSL in PowerShell with `winget install ShiningLight.OpenSSL.Light`, then reopen the terminal.)

### Run these in Git Bash
One tweak vs. before: skip the `-subj` flag (Git Bash mangles the leading `/`) and just answer the prompts.

```bash
# 1. private key — KEEP THIS FILE
openssl genrsa -out popdam_key.pem 2048

# 2. CSR — it'll prompt for fields. Set "Common Name" to: POP Creations
#    Email to your Apple ID email. Everything else can be left blank (just press Enter).
openssl req -new -key popdam_key.pem -out popdam.csr
```

Then upload `popdam.csr` to Apple (developer.apple.com → Certificates → **+** → **Developer ID Application**), download `developerID_application.cer`, drop it in the same folder, and finish:

```bash
# 3. convert + bundle into a .p12
openssl x509 -inform DER -in developerID_application.cer -out popdam_cert.pem
curl -O https://www.apple.com/certificateauthority/DeveloperIDG2CA.cer
openssl x509 -inform DER -in DeveloperIDG2CA.cer -out apple_intermediate.pem

openssl pkcs12 -export -out popdam.p12 \
  -inkey popdam_key.pem -in popdam_cert.pem \
  -certfile apple_intermediate.pem \
  -name "Developer ID Application"
# ^ prompts for an export password — remember it (that's CSC_KEY_PASSWORD)

# 4. base64 for the GitHub secret (works in Git Bash)
base64 -w0 popdam.p12 > popdam.p12.b64
```

`popdam.p12.b64` → `CSC_LINK`, and the export password → `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD`, plus the three `APPLE_*` secrets. Then run the **Publish PopDAM Helper** workflow.

Tip: to find the files afterward, they're in whatever folder Git Bash opened in — run `pwd` to see the path (default is your home folder, `C:\Users\ahazan2`).

content: Goal: get the POP DAM Helper Mac app code-signed so it installs without warnings. You're creating an Apple "Developer ID Application" certificate using OpenSSL in Git Bash. Next: open Git Bash and run the openssl key and CSR commands.
i never got an email after 'openssl req -new -key popdam_key.pem -out popdam.csr'

message.content: i never got an email after 'openssl req -new -key popdam_key.pem -out popdam.csr'

content.text:
No email is supposed to come — that step doesn't send anything. `openssl req` runs **entirely on your PC**: it just asks you those questions (Country, Common Name, Email, etc.) right there in the terminal and writes a local file called **`popdam.csr`**. Nothing goes to Apple or your inbox yet.

Confirm it worked — in Git Bash:
```bash
ls -l popdam.csr
cat popdam.csr
```
You should see the file, and `cat` should print a block starting with `-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE REQUEST-----`. If you see that, you're good.

The next step is a **website action, not email**: go to **developer.apple.com → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles → Certificates → "+" → Developer ID Application**, and when it asks you to "upload a Certificate Signing Request," pick that `popdam.csr` file. Apple then lets you **download** the certificate (`developerID_application.cer`) right there on the page — again, no email; it's an immediate download.

So the only thing that ever emails you in this whole process is unrelated (Apple's app-specific-password setup, if at all). For the cert: you make the CSR locally → upload it on Apple's site → download the .cer. Want me to walk you through the exact clicks on the Apple site once you've confirmed `popdam.csr` exists?

content.text:
In whatever folder Git Bash was "in" when you ran the command — its **current directory**. By default Git Bash opens in your home folder, so it's almost certainly:

```
C:\Users\ahazan2\popdam.csr
```

To be sure, run these in Git Bash:
```bash
pwd                      # shows the current folder, e.g. /c/Users/ahazan2  (= C:\Users\ahazan2)
ls -l popdam.csr         # confirms the file is there
explorer .               # opens that folder in Windows File Explorer
```

`pwd` prints it in Git Bash style (`/c/Users/ahazan2` means `C:\Users\ahazan2`). `explorer .` will pop open the folder so you can see `popdam.csr`, `popdam_key.pem`, etc. — and that same folder is where you'll drop the `developerID_application.cer` after downloading it from Apple, so the rest of the commands find it.

message.content: there is no link to Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles on  developer.apple.com

content.text:
It's tucked under your **Account** and only appears once you're **signed in with the enrolled (paid) developer account**. Easiest is the direct link:

👉 **https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list**

If that bounces you or looks empty, check these:

1. **Sign in first.** On developer.apple.com, click **Account** (top-right) and log in. The "Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles" area only shows *after* you're signed in — it's not on the public landing page.
2. **Membership must be active.** That section only appears if your Apple ID is **enrolled in the Apple Developer Program** ($99/yr, paid & active). A plain/free Apple ID won't show it. If you recently paid and it's "pending," it can take a few hours to a day to activate.
3. **Role.** Creating a **Developer ID Application** cert specifically requires the **Account Holder** role (the person who owns/pays for the membership). If you're an Admin or Member on someone else's team, you'll see the section but that particular cert option may be greyed out.

Once you're in and on the Certificates page, click the blue **"+"**, choose **Developer ID → Developer ID Application**, and it'll ask you to upload `popdam.csr`.

Quick check: when you go to https://developer.apple.com/account and sign in, what do you see — a dashboard with sections like "Certificates, IDs & Profiles," "App Store Connect," etc., or just a mostly-empty page / an "enroll" prompt? That'll tell us which of the three it is.

content:
AI TASK SPEC: Repository Documentation Maintenance
ROLE
You are an AI coding agent updating Markdown documentation for a software repository.
Your documentation must help future developers and future AI coding sessions understand the actual current state of the repository without relying on prior chat context.
Documentation is part of the work. It is not optional and not a final cleanup step.
update the .md files in repo: https://github.com/u2giants/seafile
________________________________________
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
Make AGENTS.md the canonical operating guide and documentation router for future developers and AI coding sessions.
AGENTS.md MUST let a new senior engineer or AI session understand the repository in under 5 minutes.
AGENTS.md MUST prevent future AI sessions from ingesting every .md file unnecessarily.
________________________________________
ABSOLUTE RULES
•	MUST derive documentation from the actual repository state.
•	MUST inspect relevant code, config, scripts, workflows, migrations, deployment files, and existing docs before editing docs.
•	MUST NOT invent architecture, intent, future plans, deployment behavior, credentials, identifiers, business rules, or undocumented conventions.
•	MUST NOT document guesses as facts.
•	MUST mark unknowns clearly.
•	MUST explain how unknowns can be verified.
•	MUST NOT include secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or production credential values.
•	MUST keep documentation high-signal and specific.
•	MUST remove or fix stale, misleading, redundant, or vague documentation.
•	MUST avoid duplicating the same guidance across multiple docs.
•	MUST update docs in the same commit as code when the code change affects docs.
•	MUST explicitly say when a change does not require documentation updates.
________________________________________
REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION FILE ROLES
Use these roles exactly:
File	Role
README.md	Quick entry point and orientation
AGENTS.md	Canonical AI/developer operating guide
CLAUDE.md	Claude Code-specific instructions only
docs/architecture.md	System design, components, data flow, constraints
docs/development.md	Local setup, run/test/lint/debug workflow
docs/configuration.md	Environment variables, config files, feature flags
docs/deployment.md	Deploy/release/environment/rollback workflow
folder-level README.md	Local context for that folder only, when genuinely useful
HANDOFF.md	Temporary continuation document for unfinished work
Create missing docs only when useful.
Delete, rewrite, or consolidate docs that are obsolete, misleading, duplicated, or too vague to help future sessions.
________________________________________
REQUIRED AGENTS.md CONTENT
AGENTS.md MUST contain the sections below.
Use actual repository paths, commands, identifiers, and deployment details.
Do not leave generic placeholders unless the value is truly unknown.
If unknown, write unknown and explain how to verify it.
________________________________________
1. Project summary
MUST include one short business/technical summary.
The summary MUST explain:
•	what the project does
•	who uses it
•	key moving parts
•	the outcome that matters
Keep this brief.
________________________________________
2. Multi-model AI note
MUST include this text near the top:
## Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
________________________________________
3. Documentation map: what to read for each task
MUST include this section near the top.
Purpose: prevent future AI sessions from loading every .md file.
Use this structure:
## Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with:

- `AGENTS.md`

Then load additional docs only when relevant:

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md`, relevant docs based on affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present, Critical incidents section in `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, relevant docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
Rules:
•	MUST be task-based.
•	MUST NOT become a flat list of every Markdown file.
•	MUST distinguish always-read docs from task-relevant docs.

message.content:
AI TASK SPEC: Repository Documentation Maintenance
ROLE
You are an AI coding agent updating Markdown documentation for a software repository.
Your documentation must help future developers and future AI coding sessions understand the actual current state of the repository without relying on prior chat context.
Documentation is part of the work. It is not optional and not a final cleanup step.
update the .md files in repo: https://github.com/u2giants/seafile
________________________________________
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
Make AGENTS.md the canonical operating guide and documentation router for future developers and AI coding sessions.
AGENTS.md MUST let a new senior engineer or AI session understand the repository in under 5 minutes.
AGENTS.md MUST prevent future AI sessions from ingesting every .md file unnecessarily.
________________________________________
ABSOLUTE RULES
•	MUST derive documentation from the actual repository state.
•	MUST inspect relevant code, config, scripts, workflows, migrations, deployment files, and existing docs before editing docs.
•	MUST NOT invent architecture, intent, future plans, deployment behavior, credentials, identifiers, business rules, or undocumented conventions.
•	MUST NOT document guesses as facts.
•	MUST mark unknowns clearly.
•	MUST explain how unknowns can be verified.
•	MUST NOT include secrets, tokens, passwords, private keys, or production credential values.
•	MUST keep documentation high-signal and specific.
•	MUST remove or fix stale, misleading, redundant, or vague documentation.
•	MUST avoid duplicating the same guidance across multiple docs.
•	MUST update docs in the same commit as code when the code change affects docs.
•	MUST explicitly say when a change does not require documentation updates.
________________________________________
REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION FILE ROLES
Use these roles exactly:
File	Role
README.md	Quick entry point and orientation
AGENTS.md	Canonical AI/developer operating guide
CLAUDE.md	Claude Code-specific instructions only
docs/architecture.md	System design, components, data flow, constraints
docs/development.md	Local setup, run/test/lint/debug workflow
docs/configuration.md	Environment variables, config files, feature flags
docs/deployment.md	Deploy/release/environment/rollback workflow
folder-level README.md	Local context for that folder only, when genuinely useful
HANDOFF.md	Temporary continuation document for unfinished work
Create missing docs only when useful.
Delete, rewrite, or consolidate docs that are obsolete, misleading, duplicated, or too vague to help future sessions.
________________________________________
REQUIRED AGENTS.md CONTENT
AGENTS.md MUST contain the sections below.
Use actual repository paths, commands, identifiers, and deployment details.
Do not leave generic placeholders unless the value is truly unknown.
If unknown, write unknown and explain how to verify it.
________________________________________
1. Project summary
MUST include one short business/technical summary.
The summary MUST explain:
•	what the project does
•	who uses it
•	key moving parts
•	the outcome that matters
Keep this brief.
________________________________________
2. Multi-model AI note
MUST include this text near the top:
## Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
________________________________________
3. Documentation map: what to read for each task
MUST include this section near the top.
Purpose: prevent future AI sessions from loading every .md file.
Use this structure:
## Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with:

- `AGENTS.md`

Then load additional docs only when relevant:

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md`, relevant docs based on affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present, Critical incidents section in `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, relevant docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
Rules:
•	MUST be task-based.
•	MUST NOT become a flat list of every Markdown file.
•	MUST distinguish always-read docs from task-relevant docs.
•	MUST identify docs that are usually unnecessary.
•	MUST mention HANDOFF.md as required reading when it exists.

content.text: The repo is already substantially documented (and recent commits show a prior session did exactly this kind of refresh). My job is to **verify against actual state, fix what's stale, and fill any gaps** per the spec. Let me read the core docs first.

content.content:
1	# AGENTS.md — POP Creations Seafile Infrastructure
2	
3	## What This Project Is
4	
5	POP Creations runs a 28TB design file library on two Synology NAS devices in a NYC office. Eight graphic designers in São Paulo need access to that library over the internet. This repo contains all infrastructure configuration to make that work:
6	
7	1. **Seafile Pro** (image `seafileltd/seafile-pro-mc:13.0-latest`, a rolling tag that auto-tracks the newest 13.0.x patch) running on a Linode VPS (`seafile.designflow.app`) — the relay server. NAS pushes files here via seaf-cli; designers pull via HTTPS/desktop app.
8	2. **seaf-cli Docker containers** on the Synology NAS — continuously sync NAS folders to specific Seafile libraries. Built from a wrapper image in `synology-seaf-cli/` (the one thing this repo builds and publishes to GHCR).
9	3. **nas-settings** — a Flask panel on the VPS (`/nas-settings/`) that gives the Seafile web UI a GUI for the seaf-cli client on the NAS: live status, sync controls (pause/resume/restart/stop), daemon config, and library management (list/list-remote/create/desync), plus the ingest window. The server can't reach the NAS, so it queues commands by library UUID and the containers pick them up on their 30 s status poll.
10	4. File data is stored in **Linode Object Storage (São Paulo, br-gru-1)**, not on the VPS disk. The VPS only holds metadata (MariaDB), session cache (Redis), and config.
11	
12	This is mostly an **infrastructure configuration repo**. Two images are CI-built and published to GHCR: the seaf-cli wrapper (`.github/workflows/seaf-cli-image.yml`) and the nas-settings panel (`.github/workflows/nas-settings-image.yml`). Everything else runs upstream images via Docker Compose — the deployment artifacts are the compose files and scripts themselves.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Multi-model AI note
17	
18	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
19	
20	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
21	
22	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
23	
24	(`.cursorignore` exists for Cursor with matching content; there is no `.copilotignore` — Copilot is not used here.)
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Repository Structure
29	
30	```
31	seafile-repo/                    Root — GitHub: github.com/u2giants/seafile
32	├── AGENTS.md                    ← You are here. Read before touching anything.
33	├── CLAUDE.md                    Claude Code-specific instructions
34	├── HANDOFF.md                   In-progress work notes (delete when NAS sync + designer accounts done)
35	├── README.md                    Project overview for GitHub
36	├── .gitignore                   Excludes .env, CREDENTIALS.txt, *.sql
37	├── .claudeignore
38	├── .cursorignore
39	│
40	├── .github/workflows/
41	│   ├── seaf-cli-image.yml       CI: lint + test + build + push the seaf-cli wrapper image to GHCR
42	│   └── nas-settings-image.yml   CI: lint + test + build + push the nas-settings panel image to GHCR
43	│
44	├── seafile-server/              VPS configuration — live at seafile.designflow.app
45	│   ├── seafile-server.yml       Docker Compose: Seafile Pro + MariaDB + Redis
46	│   ├── caddy.yml                Docker Compose: Caddy reverse proxy + TLS
47	│   ├── nas-settings.yml         Docker Compose: nas-settings Flask panel
48	│   ├── .env.example             Template — never commit the real .env
49	│   ├── START_SEAFILE.sh         Pre-flight checks + start command
50	│   ├── CONFIGURE_OAUTH.sh       One-time OAuth setup (already run — DO NOT RUN AGAIN)
51	│   ├── CREATE_NAS_SYNC_ACCOUNT.sh  One-time machine account creation (already run — DO NOT RUN AGAIN)
52	│   ├── nas-settings/            Flask app (app.py + templates) — seaf-cli control panel + status/command API (test_app.py)
53	│   │   └── Dockerfile           Built + published by CI to ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:nas-settings-latest
54	│   ├── custom-templates/        Seahub template override — injects the nas-settings link into the sysadmin sidebar
55	│   └── docs/                    seafile-server component docs (see "Documentation map" below)
56	│       ├── architecture.md      Containers, networking, storage architecture
57	│       ├── configuration.md     All env vars and config file contents
58	│       ├── deployment.md        Start/stop, updates, backup, NAS image releases
59	│       └── development.md       Logs, debugging, API usage, user management
60	│
61	├── synology-seaf-cli/           NAS sync containers — wrapper image source
62	│   ├── Dockerfile               Wrapper image — FROM flrnnc/seafile-client:latest, adds tini + fixed entrypoints
63	│   ├── entrypoint.py            Fixed Seafile daemon entrypoint (replaces image default) + healthcheck + status reporter & command dispatcher (test_entrypoint.py)
64	│   ├── seaf-entrypoint.py       Hardlink/scandir date-filter staging wrapper; launches entrypoint.py
65	│   ├── docker-compose.yml       One service per Seafile library
66	│   ├── .env.example             NAS sync password template
67	│   └── README.md                Synology setup + redeploy instructions
68	│
69	└── windows-workstation/         Alternative seaf-cli host (NOT active) — Windows rendering machine
70	    ├── docker-compose.yml       seaf-cli containers (sources via CIFS from NAS)
71	    ├── setup.ps1                One-shot installer: PopDAM agent + Docker + seaf-cli
72	    └── README.md                Machine replacement instructions for Albert
73	```
74	
75	All files in this repo are **authored by this project** — there are no third-party packages or vendor directories. The two things built from upstream bases are the seaf-cli wrapper (`FROM flrnnc/seafile-client:latest`) and `nas-settings` (a Flask app); both layer our code on top rather than modifying vendor code in-repo.
76	
77	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
78	
79	Always start with:
80	
81	- `AGENTS.md` (this file)
82	- `HANDOFF.md` **if it exists** (in-progress work — required reading before continuing anything)
83	
84	Then load additional docs only when relevant — do **not** ingest every `.md` file:
85	
86	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
87	|---|---|---|
88	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | `seafile-server/docs/*`; `lucid.md` (storage-strategy research notes) |
89	| Modify project-owned code (nas-settings panel, seaf-cli wrapper) | `AGENTS.md`, the relevant folder README (`seafile-server/nas-settings/README.md` or `synology-seaf-cli/README.md`), `seafile-server/docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `seafile-server/docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behaviour changes |
90	| Change configuration, env vars, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `seafile-server/docs/configuration.md`, `seafile-server/docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | unrelated architecture docs |
91	| Change local dev / test / lint / debug workflow | `AGENTS.md`, `seafile-server/docs/development.md`, the relevant `test_*.py` and workflow files | `seafile-server/docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
92	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, release, rollback, runtime env | `AGENTS.md` → Deployment, `seafile-server/docs/deployment.md`, `.github/workflows/*`, the relevant compose file | local-only dev docs |
93	| Change Seafile accounts, libraries, OAuth, UUIDs, or other identifiers | `AGENTS.md` → Data Model + Idiosyncratic Decisions, `seafile-server/docs/architecture.md`, `seafile-server/docs/configuration.md` if env affected | deployment docs unless rollout changes |
94	| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md` → Critical Incident Log + Idiosyncratic Decisions, `HANDOFF.md` if present, the doc for the affected area | unrelated folder READMEs |
95	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, the docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
96	| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder README, only the broader docs it references | other folder READMEs |
97	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless the task needs them |
98	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `seafile-server/docs/`, folder READMEs where relevant | source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
99	
100	There is intentionally **no top-level `docs/`** — component docs live beside the component they describe (`seafile-server/docs/`, folder READMEs). Do not create a parallel top-level `docs/` tree; it would duplicate `seafile-server/docs/`.
101	
102	---
103	
104	## The Prime Directive
105	
106	**Our configuration lives in this repo.** All changes to the Seafile infrastructure go through files in this repo, committed to `main`, then applied on the VPS or NAS. Never make changes directly on the server and consider them done — changes made only on the server will be lost or overwritten and leave the repo out of sync with reality.
107	
108	**Scripts that say "DO NOT RUN AGAIN" are off-limits.** `CONFIGURE_OAUTH.sh` and `CREATE_NAS_SYNC_ACCOUNT.sh` have already been run against the live system. Running them again creates duplicates. See Idiosyncratic Decisions.
109	
110	### Custom-code boundary
111	
112	Everything in this repo is **project-owned** — there are no vendor, generated, or framework trees. Project logic lives in:
113	
114	- `seafile-server/nas-settings/` — the Flask control panel (`app.py`, `templates/`, `test_app.py`)
115	- `synology-seaf-cli/` — the seaf-cli wrapper image (`Dockerfile`, `entrypoint.py`, `seaf-entrypoint.py`, `docker-compose.yml`, `test_entrypoint.py`)
116	- `seafile-server/*.yml`, `seafile-server/*.sh` — VPS Docker Compose + operational scripts
117	- `seafile-server/custom-templates/` — the **only** Seahub overrides (full-copy templates + an injected `<script>`; see Core Modification Inventory)
118	- `.github/workflows/` — CI
119	- docs: `*.md`, `seafile-server/docs/`
120	
121	Everything **outside** the repo — Seafile/Seahub source, the `flrnnc/seafile-client` base image, and runtime config/state on the hosts (`seahub_settings.py`, `/opt/seafile/.env`, the Seafile database) — is upstream or runtime. Do not scatter project logic into it. Seahub UI changes go through `custom-templates/` (volume-mounted), never by editing Seafile's installed files. Runtime/auth config changes are made on the host (they have no repo representation) and noted here — see Idiosyncratic Decisions and the CI/CD §25 exception.
122	
123	---
124	
125	## Core Modification Inventory
126	
127	This project is original infrastructure config (not a fork). No upstream source files are edited in-repo. The only place we override upstream behavior is a Seahub template, layered via a volume mount (not a code edit):
128	
129	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
130	|------|-------------|----------------------|----------------------|
131	| `seafile-server/custom-templates/sysadmin/sysadmin_react_app.html` | Full copy of Seahub's sysadmin template + injected `<script>` adding a "NAS Sync Settings" link to the **System Admin** sidebar | Seafile has no plugin hook to add a custom admin page link | A Seafile UI upgrade can change the upstream template; re-diff against `/opt/seafile/seafile-pro-server-<ver>/seahub/seahub/templates/sysadmin/sysadmin_react_app.html` after major upgrades. |
132	| `seafile-server/custom-templates/react_app.html` | Full copy of Seahub's main-app template + injected **admin-only** (`{% if user.is_staff %}`) `<script>` adding a "NAS Sync" link to the **main workspace** sidebar (`.side-nav-con .nav-container`) | Lets admins reach the panel from the normal Seafile UI without typing the URL; non-admins never see a dead link | Same upgrade risk — re-diff against `…/seahub/seahub/templates/react_app.html` (baseline 13.0.21). Validate it compiles before activating (see `custom-templates/README.md`); a syntax error breaks the main page for everyone after the next Seahub reload. |
133	
134	---
135	
136	## Decision Tree
137	
138	### If you need to change a Seafile configuration setting:
139	- Edit the relevant file in `/opt/seafile-data/seafile/conf/` on the VPS
140	- Copy the updated content into `seafile-server/docs/configuration.md` in this repo
141	- Commit to main, push
142	
143	### If you need to add a new NAS folder sync:
144	1. Identify the NAS path (ask Albert — paths are case-sensitive)
145	2. Create a Seafile library via admin UI or API; record the UUID
146	3. Add a new service block to `synology-seaf-cli/docker-compose.yml` following the existing pattern (and add the library to `LIBRARIES` in `seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py` so it shows in the panel)
147	4. Commit + push; recreate the seaf-cli stack on **edgesynology1 over SSH** (the NAS MCP is read-only — see Deployment / `synology-seaf-cli/README.md`)
148	5. Update the Container Inventory in this file
149	6. Commit and push
150	
151	### If you need to update the Seafile server version:
152	- The image is pinned to the rolling tag `seafileltd/seafile-pro-mc:13.0-latest`, so a plain `docker compose pull seafile && docker compose up -d --force-recreate seafile` already moves to the newest **13.0.x patch**. No file change is needed for patch updates.
153	- To move to a new **minor/major** (e.g. a future 13.1 or 14.0): check https://manual.seafile.com for breaking changes, edit `SEAFILE_IMAGE` in `/opt/seafile/.env` and the default in `seafile-server/seafile-server.yml`, commit, then pull + recreate. Watch `docker logs -f seafile`.
154	- As of 2026-06: 13.0 is the current major; the server tracks it automatically.
155	
156	### If you need to deploy or change the nas-settings panel:
157	- Code lives in `seafile-server/nas-settings/` (Flask `app.py` + templates); compose in `seafile-server/nas-settings.yml`. Commit to `main` → CI (`nas-settings image`) tests + builds + publishes `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:nas-settings-latest` (+ `:nas-settings-sha-<commit>`). Deploy by **pulling** that image on the VPS — do not `docker compose build` on the host (§25 exception model).

271	## What to Ignore
272	
273	This is a lean config-only repo — there are no `node_modules/`, `dist/`, build outputs, vendored SDKs, or third-party trees to skip. The only things that should never enter the repo or your context:
274	
275	- `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc` — Python bytecode (git-ignored)
276	- `.env`, `CREDENTIALS.txt`, `*.sql` — secrets / DB dumps (git-ignored; never commit, never paste)
277	
278	For **context economy**, don't ingest every doc — use the Documentation map above. In particular, `lucid.md` is standalone storage-strategy research (LucidLink/JuiceFS/Resilio evaluation) and is **not** needed for operating or changing this system; load it only if the task is specifically about that storage strategy.
279	
280	These match `.claudeignore` / `.cursorignore` (which carry only a comment, since there are no large dirs to exclude) and `.gitignore`.
